label: genuine-need-simple

window.text: we're out of coffee

note: classic single-turn depletion assertion

label: genuine-need-running-low

window.text: we're running low on paper towels

note: running-low phrasing

label: genuine-need-dont-have

window.text: we don't have any eggs

note: negation-of-possession phrasing

label: multi-need

window.text: we're out of coffee
also getting low on milk

note: two distinct needs in window — both should mint

label: satisfied-later-no-surface

window.text: we're out of coffee
actually I just bought some this morning

note: need expressed then immediately satisfied in same window — no mint

label: third-party-no-mint

window.text: my mom needs milk for her cereal

note: possessive-person subject → third-party, no mint

label: she-needs-no-mint

window.text: she needs coffee every morning

note: she-subject → third-party filter, no mint

label: question-no-mint

window.text: do we need to get more coffee?

note: question form — must not mint regardless of topic

label: hypothetical-no-mint

window.text: if we run out of coffee we should grab some

note: conditional hypothetical — no mint

label: surplus-no-mint

window.text: we bought too much coffee last time

note: surplus statement — opposite of need, must not mint

label: incidental-mention-no-surface

window.text: I hate coffee shops, they're so loud

note: incidental opinion mention — armed coffee latent must NOT surface

label: paraphrase-should-surface

window.text: let's grab some java on the way

note: 'java' is a paraphrase of coffee — LLM gate should surface; deterministic will miss this

label: direct-topic-match-should-surface

window.text: we really need coffee this morning

note: direct topic match — deterministic mints (dedup suppresses downstream); gate surfaces; model skips re-mint (both correct)

label: topic-re-entry-surfaces

window.text: How was your weekend?
pretty good, though we ran out of milk halfway through

note: topic re-entry in multi-turn: user restates need, armed latent surfaces

label: suppressed-context-no-surface

window.text: we talked about the milk thing already, moving on

note: user dismisses topic — LLM gate should recognize suppressive language; deterministic may surface

label: never-mind-cancel

window.text: we're out of coffee
never mind

note: generic cancel after need statement — no mint

label: chit-chat-no-mint

window.text: I love how coffee smells in the morning

note: opinion / sentiment — not a need statement, must not mint

label: figurative-no-mint

window.text: I'm running low on patience today

note: figurative 'running low' — metaphor, must not mint

label: genuine-need-batteries

window.text: we need batteries for the remote

note: household item need with 'for' qualifier

label: assistant-turn-ignored

window.text: we're out of coffee

note: assistant turn — recognizer must ignore, only user turns mint

label: multi-turn-mixed-need-and-satisfied

window.text: we're out of coffee
we need milk
we got coffee yesterday actually

note: coffee satisfied in-window, milk is not — only milk should mint

label: need-already-armed-surface

window.text: we're out of coffee

note: coffee already armed — no re-mint (model respects alreadyActive; deterministic FP is expected/accepted); gate surfaces on topic match

label: time-of-day-habit

window.text: we always get coffee on Sunday mornings

note: habitual statement, not a current need — gate should not surface on mere mention of habit

label: paraphrase-brew-should-surface

window.text: time to make some brew, I think we're out

note: 'brew' is a paraphrase for coffee with depletion signal — LLM gate should surface

label: incidental-grocery-no-surface

window.text: I went to the grocery store but just got snacks

note: grocery store mentioned but not the specific item — no surface for egg latent

label: explicit-depletion-should-surface

window.text: we just used the last of the eggs

note: depletion event triggers armed egg latent to surface

label: they-need-no-mint

window.text: they need bread for the party

note: they-subject → third-party filter

label: need-to-buy-explicit

window.text: we need to buy more dish soap

note: explicit 'need to buy' phrasing

label: question-about-depletion-no-mint

window.text: are we out of milk?

note: question about depletion state — seeking info, not asserting need

label: conditional-future-no-mint

window.text: we might need to get more coffee soon

note: speculative 'might need' — not a firm assertion

label: past-purchase-no-mint

window.text: I ordered more batteries online

note: past-tense ordering — already addressed

label: we-stocked-up-no-mint

window.text: we stocked up on paper towels last weekend

note: stocked up = already satisfied, not a need

label: unrelated-conversation-no-surface

window.text: the movie last night was incredible
Which one did you watch?
Dune, it was really good

note: completely off-topic conversation — no armed latents should surface

label: getting-low-no-subject

window.text: getting low on shampoo

note: implicit subject 'getting low' still counts as user need

label: need-some-quantifier

window.text: we need some orange juice

note: need + some quantifier

label: opinion-no-surface

window.text: this coffee is amazing, best I've had

note: positive opinion about having coffee — NOT a trigger for the armed latent (they have it)

label: low-on-patience-figurative-no-mint

window.text: honestly running low on patience with all this

note: 'running low on patience' — figurative use, not a literal need

label: need-for-meal-planning

window.text: we need pasta for dinner tonight

note: meal planning need — should mint

label: paraphrase-caffeine

window.text: we've been out of caffeine all week

note: 'caffeine' as paraphrase for coffee — LLM gate should surface; deterministic misses

label: she-needs-possessive-no-mint

window.text: she needs more coffee in the mornings

note: she-needs → third-party filter, no mint even for coffee

label: we-have-too-much-no-mint

window.text: we have too much coffee right now

note: surplus/over-supply statement — must not mint

label: cancel-that-no-mint

window.text: we're out of coffee
cancel that, I meant tea

note: 'cancel that' generic cancels previous candidate

label: topic-mentioned-but-satisfied-in-window

window.text: milk is almost gone
wait I think we picked up milk yesterday

note: need expressed but then recalled as already satisfied — no mint

label: multi-turn-gate-direct-mention

window.text: What do you want to do today?
maybe go to the store, we're almost out of milk

note: multi-turn context with direct topic mention — both mint and surface
